creator: chogata@moosefs.com
text:
I tested atime change with futimens call from userland (FreeBSD 13.2) and it did not send ctime change (no ctime flag on in setattr, both the setattr after read and the setattr caused by futimens call have 0x10). So I'm back to the starting point: no way to say if setattr is a result of internal atime change after a read operation or a user-made operation.

(I'm leaving on 2 weeks vacation tomorrow, so I won't be able to follow here for a while).
